textPlain: This is a feature, not a bug.
Trump admin are fast tracking nuclear, in order to shorten the energy gap between us and China energy output.Heavily subsidised solar is neat and all, but you need a bulldozer here, not a shovel.
I'm guessing the US/foreign oil companies paid enough bribe for Trump to do this?
Luddites having their fun. I wish them to fail soon.

“We will not approve wind or farmer destroying Solar,” Trump posted on Truth Social Wednesday. “The days of stupidity are over in the USA!!!”It's just so self fulfilling. Someone is being very stupid. Maybe it's me. Maybe it's him. Possibly it's both of us.But there is no way out of the conclusion that the country is (at least) a near majority of very stupid people. Not merely mistaken or holding different values, but genuinely incapable of distinguishing reality from fantasy.Of course if I'm the stupid one, maybe my reasoning there is also wrong. I know I conflated individuals and groups in a less-than-rigorous fashion.But either way I feel unable to find a solution to this conundrum. Certainly I can't if I'm stupid. And if a majority of voters are stupid, then it undercuts the principle that enables us to live in the same region together. I am rapidly running out of ideas to continue that.

Yea all the “this bad thing will happen” discussion misses that the intent has been plain for at least a year now. The administration has plainly said what they would do during the election and they have rather faithfully executed on that plan. This isn’t about fixing things or saving people money it’s about doing what they want to do.
Exactly, see Enron. Once you control the production and can manipulate the price you then extract obscene profits.
Like any project you need both.

Depressingly, the real answer is probably even worse. It’s not like there aren’t rich companies who won’t suffer from the upcoming power crunches and would write big checks to make them not happen. But that won’t work.No, the actual answer is that it’s pure ideology: this is about Owning the Libs, pure and simple, irrespective of the collateral damage to the economy. We’re currently being led by vengeance-minded morons who want to destroy anything that has even a tangential connection to liberal politics, and renewables are definitely on that list.

Could it be as much as the amounts that the renewable companies paid to the last administration?You might prefer certain types of energy, but you would be naive if you didn't recognize the cash flows from all the different types of companies.

I think about one third who heard what they wanted to hear in his campaign promises, one third completely against and one third who consider themselves "apolitical".With any luck the midterm elections will allow sanity to return.
